x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces NYC Aging's open provider network (providers, older adult centers, activities, service units, participation) and adds make_referral — the net-new write workflow locked today inside the phone-based Aging Connect intake.

server.name: io.nyc.dfta
server.title: NYC Aging (DFTA)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Department for the Aging (DFTA / 'NYC Aging') — DFTA-funded service providers, older adult centers (senior centers) and their activities, contract service units, and aggregate participation from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to make and track a service referral that today lives only in the phone-based Aging Connect intake.
server.instructions: Use find_providers / get_provider to locate DFTA-funded providers by borough, provider type (older adult center, case management, home-delivered meals, caregiver, NORC, legal, transportation), or council district. find_older_adult_centers returns senior centers with Local Law 140 operating profiles (utilization, meals, staffing). find_activities lists what is happening at older adult centers. find_service_units answers contract budget/performance questions; find_participation returns AGGREGATE unduplicated client counts only — DFTA never exposes an individual older adult. make_referral (net-new) connects an older adult to services; it requires an authenticated user and mutates state — confirm the service, the older adult's borough/ZIP, and the details with the user before submitting, and set urgent=true for suspected elder abuse, no food, or an unsafe-at-home situation so it routes immediately to Aging Connect / Adult Protective Services.

tools.name: find_providers
tools.title: Find Providers
tools.description: Search DFTA-funded service providers by borough, provider type, funding, council district, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_provider
tools.title: Get Provider
tools.description: Fetch a DFTA-funded service provider (sponsor, type, hours, address, geography) by DFTA ID.

tools.name: find_older_adult_centers
tools.title: Find Older Adult Centers
tools.description: List older adult centers (senior centers) with their Local Law 140 operating profiles — utilization, meals, staffing — by borough or site type.

tools.name: find_activities
tools.title: Find Activities
tools.description: Search older adult center activities and events by borough, event type, host center (DFTA ID), virtual/in-person, or free text.

tools.name: find_service_units
tools.title: Find Service Units
tools.description: Search budgeted-and-reported contract service units (budgeted vs. reported units and amounts) by provider (DFTA ID), provider type, service name, or contract year.

tools.name: find_participation
tools.title: Find Participation
tools.description: Return AGGREGATE participation — unduplicated client counts — by program/site, program type, fiscal year, or borough. Never returns individual older adults.

tools.name: list_my_referrals
tools.title: List My Referrals
tools.description: List the authenticated user's own service referrals and their status — today trackable only by phone to Aging Connect.

tools.name: make_referral
tools.title: Make a Referral
tools.description: Create a NYC Aging service referral (connect an older adult to services). NET-NEW workflow with no public API today — it lives only in the phone-based Aging Connect intake (212-AGING-NYC) or a 311 call. Requires an authenticated user. Mutates state — confirm the service, the older adult's borough/ZIP, and the details first; set urgent=true for suspected elder abuse, no food, or an unsafe-at-home situation.
